  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/queue.go", "symbol": "TypedInterface"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/delaying_queue.go", "symbol": "TypedDelayingInterface"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go", "symbol": "TypedRateLimitingInterface"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go", "symbol": "RateLimitingInterface"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/rate_limiting_queue.go", "symbol": "rateLimitingType"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "TypedRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "TypedBucketRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "BucketRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "TypedMaxOfRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "MaxOfRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "TypedItemFastSlowRateLimiter"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue/default_rate_limiters.go", "symbol": "DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go", "symbol": "Controller.Run"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go", "symbol": "Controller.runWorker"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go", "symbol": "Controller.processNextWorkItem"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0", "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go", "symbol": "Controller.syncHandler"}
  ],
  "text": "The Kubernetes controller work queue with rate limiting is implemented across multiple packages in the client-go library (staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue). The architecture consists of four layered interfaces:\n\n1. **Base Queue Interface (queue.go)**: TypedInterface defines core queue operations: Add(item), Get() returns (item, shutdown), Done(item), Len(), ShutDown(), and ShuttingDown(). This is implemented using a simple FIFO queue (queue[T] which is a slice).\n\n2. **Delaying Queue Layer (delaying_queue.go)**: TypedDelayingInterface extends TypedInterface by adding AddAfter(item, duration) for delayed requeuing. This is implemented by delayingType which wraps the base queue and uses a goroutine (waitingLoop) to handle delayed items with a priority heap.\n\n3. **Rate Limiting Queue Layer (rate_limiting_queue.go)**: TypedRateLimitingInterface extends TypedDelayingInterface with AddRateLimited(item), Forget(item), and NumRequeues(item). The implementation (rateLimitingType) wraps a TypedDelayingInterface and uses a TypedRateLimiter to determine when items should be added back to the queue.\n\n4. **Rate Limiter Strategies (default_rate_limiters.go)**: Multiple rate limiting strategies are available:\n   - TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter: Implements exponential backoff (baseDelay * 2^num_failures) with a maximum delay cap. Tracks failure counts per item in a mutex-protected map.\n   - TypedBucketRateLimiter: Uses golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter for token bucket rate limiting. Returns 0 requeues (no per-item state).\n   - TypedMaxOfRateLimiter: Composes multiple rate limiters and returns the worst-case (maximum) delay.\n   - TypedItemFastSlowRateLimiter: Allows a configurable number of fast retries (fastDelay) before switching to slow retries (slowDelay).\n   - DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter: Combines ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter (5ms base, 1000s max) with BucketRateLimiter (10 qps, 100 bucket) using MaxOfRateLimiter.\n\nThe **Controller Reconcile Loop Pattern** is consistently implemented across Kubernetes controllers:\n1. Controller.Run() starts worker goroutines that call wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, controller.runWorker, ...)\n2. runWorker() calls processNextWorkItem() in a loop until it returns false\n3. processNextWorkItem() calls queue.Get() to retrieve an item, defers queue.Done(item), executes syncHandler()\n4. On success, calls queue.Forget(item); on error, calls queue.AddRateLimited(item) for retry\n5. The queue automatically applies rate limiting delays before returning items from Get()\n\nKey controller implementations demonstrating this pattern include:\n- staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go: Complete example with event handlers and syncHandler\n- pkg/controller/job/job_controller.go: Complex controller with multiple queue types (queue, orphanQueue)\n- pkg/controller/bootstrap/tokencleaner.go: Simple pattern with Get/Done/AddRateLimited\n- pkg/controller/certificates/certificate_controller.go: Certificate lifecycle management\n- pkg/controller/namespace/namespace_controller.go: Uses NewTypedMaxOfRateLimiter with both exponential and bucket limiters\n- pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go: Multiple worker goroutines processing service and topology queues\n- staging/src/k8s.io/cloud-provider/controllers/service/controller.go: Cloud provider service reconciliation\n- staging/src/k8s.io/cloud-provider/controllers/node/node_controller.go: Node synchronization pattern\n\nThe system ensures that work items are processed exactly once at a time (via queue.Get/Done guarantees), transient failures trigger automatic requeue with exponential backoff, and overall controller throughput is rate-limited to prevent API server overload."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Kubernetes Controller Manager Work Queue and Rate Limiting

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the controller work queue with rate limiting: the RateLimitingInterface, the ItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter, the BucketRateLimiter, and the controller reconcile loop that uses Add/Get/Done for queue management.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the controller work queue with rate limiting. Let me start by exploring the workspace and then search systematically for these components.
Now let me create a comprehensive summary file with all the findings.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive search and analysis of the Kubernetes controller work queue with rate limiting implementation. Here's what I found:

## Summary

I've created `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed findings about the work queue and rate limiting system in kubernetes/kubernetes v1.32.0.

### Key Findings:

**Core Workqueue Architecture (4-layer design):**
1. **TypedInterface** (queue.go) - Basic FIFO queue with Add/Get/Done operations
2. **TypedDelayingInterface** (delaying_queue.go) - Adds AddAfter() for delayed requeuing
3. **TypedRateLimitingInterface** (rate_limiting_queue.go) - Adds AddRateLimited() with pluggable rate limiting
4. **Rate Limiter Strategies** (default_rate_limiters.go) - Multiple implementations

**Rate Limiting Implementations:**
- **TypedItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter** - Exponential backoff per item (baseDelay * 2^failures)
- **TypedBucketRateLimiter** - Token bucket rate limiting (10 qps, 100 burst default)
- **TypedMaxOfRateLimiter** - Composes multiple limiters, returns worst-case delay
- **TypedItemFastSlowRateLimiter** - Fast retries then slow retries

**Controller Reconcile Loop Pattern:**
```
Run() → runWorker() → processNextWorkItem() {
  item, shutdown := queue.Get()
  defer queue.Done(item)
  
  err := syncHandler(item)
  if err != nil {
    queue.AddRateLimited(item)  // Auto rate-limited retry
  } else {
    queue.Forget(item)  // Clear retry state
  }
}
```

**20 Key Files** across the codebase including:
- Core workqueue package files
- Sample controller (reference implementation)
- Multiple production controllers (Job, Certificate, Namespace, EndpointSlice, etc.)

The answer.json file contains complete details with file paths, symbol definitions, and the reconcile loop chain.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0$ controller work queue reconcile loop Add Get Done

On success, calls queue.Forget(item); on error, calls queue.AddRateLimited(item) for retry\n5. The queue automatically applies rate limiting delays before returning items from Get()\n\nKey controller implementations demonstrating this pattern include:\n- staging/src/k8s.io/sample-controller/controller.go: Complete example with event handlers and syncHandler\n- pkg/controller/job/job_controller.go: Complex controller with multiple queue types (queue, orphanQueue)\n- pkg/controller/bootstrap/tokencleaner.go: Simple pattern with Get/Done/AddRateLimited\n- pkg/controller/certificates/certificate_controller.go: Certificate lifecycle management\n- pkg/controller/namespace/namespace_controller.go: Uses NewTypedMaxOfRateLimiter with both exponential and bucket limiters\n- pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go: Multiple worker goroutines processing service and topology queues\n- staging/src/k8s.io/cloud-provider/controllers/service/controller.go: Cloud provider service reconciliation\n- staging/src/k8s.io/cloud-provider/controllers/node/node_controller.go: Node synchronization pattern\n\nThe system ensures that work items are processed exactly once at a time (via queue.Get/Done guarantees), transient failures trigger automatic requeue with exponential backoff, and overall controller throughput is rate-limited to prevent API server overload."
